name: Tehran — Khamenei Compound
details: Opening decapitation strike destroyed Supreme Leader Khamenei's residential compound. Khamenei killed along with 4 family members. ~40-50 senior officials killed across simultaneous Tehran strikes.
casualties: 5 killed at compound; ~50 officials across Tehran

name: Tehran — IRGC Malek-Ashtar HQ
details: IRGC Malek-Ashtar building completely destroyed in joint US-Israel missile strike. Thousands of IRGC personnel killed or wounded across military bases.

name: Tehran — IRIB State Broadcaster HQ
details: Israeli air operation struck the headquarters of Iran's state broadcaster (IRIB) in Tehran. No casualties reported at this specific site.

name: Tehran — Parliament Building
details: Iranian state-linked media indicated parliament building was targeted by airstrikes. Israel also bombed the Assembly of Experts during a meeting to elect the next supreme leader.

name: Tehran — Khatam-al-Anbia Hospital
details: Hospital struck in Tehran, verified by BBC. Gandhi Hospital also hit. Medical facilities damaged during ongoing strikes.

name: Tehran — Mehrabad Airport
details: Mehrabad Airport in Tehran struck, destroying military aircraft including F-14 Tomcats at airbases.

name: Tehran — Basij & Police Sites
details: Multiple Basij bases, police stations (104 Abbas Abad, 113 near 15 Khordad Square), Vali Asr garrison, and special police units destroyed across Tehran.

name: Isfahan — Nuclear & Missile Facilities
details: Key hub for Iran's ballistic missile programme targeted. IAEA confirmed damage to two buildings at the nuclear facility. Production plants and research facilities hit.

name: Qom — Military Headquarters
details: Military headquarters in the holy city of Qom targeted in initial wave of strikes.

name: Karaj — Military Targets
details: Military installations near Karaj, west of Tehran, struck in initial wave of joint US-Israeli operation.

name: Kermanshah — Military Targets
details: Military facilities in western Iran targeted in initial strikes.

name: Minab — Girls' School Airstrike
details: Shajareh Tayyebeh girls' elementary school struck. Building collapsed on students and teachers. Deadliest single civilian incident of the conflict. US and Israel deny responsibility. Iran blames the coalition. Mass funeral held Mar 4.

name: Tabriz — Police Building
details: Police building struck in Tabriz, northwestern Iran. Part of widespread strikes across 24 of Iran's 31 provinces.

name: Shiraz — Air Force Base
details: Iranian Air Force base in Shiraz attacked. Military aircraft destroyed on the ground.

name: Bandar Abbas — Military Targets
details: Military targets in the strategic port city of Bandar Abbas, near the Strait of Hormuz, struck by coalition forces.

name: Mashhad — Military Targets
details: Military installations in Iran's second-largest city targeted in strikes extending across northeastern Iran.

name: Ahvaz — Military Targets
details: Military facilities in southwestern Iran's Ahvaz struck during multi-province operations.

name: Lamerd — Sports Hall
details: A sports hall in Lamerd was bombed during a girls' practice session. 21 civilians killed including young athletes.

name: Hamadan — Military Targets
details: Military installations in Hamadan province struck as part of widespread multi-province strikes.

name: Bushehr — Nuclear Area
details: Explosions heard near Bushehr nuclear power plant perimeter. Russia warned of threat to nuclear safety. IAEA expressed concern.

name: Beit Shemesh — Synagogue/Shelter
details: Iranian ballistic missile directly struck a residential area, destroying a synagogue and public bomb shelter beneath it. Deadliest single strike on Israel during the conflict.

name: Tel Aviv — Residential Block
details: Complete Iranian ballistic missile with warhead of several hundred kg struck next to an apartment building, creating a large crater. ~40 buildings sustained damage.

name: Bnei Brak — Aleh Campus & School
details: Iranian missile directly struck the Aleh special education campus and a nearby school. Severe structural damage.

name: Rosh Ha'ayin — Public Building
details: Iranian missile struck a public building simultaneously with the Beit Shemesh attack.

name: Ramat Gan
details: Impact area during Iranian missile barrage. Injuries from people rushing to shelters.

name: Haifa Area — Interceptions
details: Continuous sirens and explosions in skies above. Interceptor activity overhead. Missile fragments and debris fell.

name: Bahrain — US 5th Fleet HQ (Juffair)
details: Iranian missiles targeted the headquarters of the US Navy's 5th Fleet in Bahrain's Juffair area multiple times. 45 missiles and 9 drones intercepted. Bahrain airport also targeted by drone.

name: Bahrain — Crowne Plaza Hotel, Manama
details: An Iranian drone hit the Crowne Plaza Hotel in Manama, causing a fire.

name: Kuwait — Ali Al Salem Air Base
details: Ballistic missiles targeted Ali Al Salem Air Base (hosting US and Italian forces). Kuwait intercepted 97 ballistic missiles and 283 drones total. Kuwait International Airport also hit by drone.
casualties: 1 girl killed from shrapnel, 3+ others killed

name: Kuwait — Port Shuaiba (US Soldiers)
details: Iranian drone attack killed 4 US Army Reserve soldiers from the 103rd Sustainment Command (Des Moines, Iowa). 2 additional soldiers killed at regional facility.

name: Qatar — Al Udeid Air Base
details: Iran targeted Al Udeid Air Base and LNG facilities. Qatar intercepted 18 ballistic missiles, cruise missiles, and drones. LNG production halted after drone strikes on facilities.

name: UAE — Al Dhafra Air Base / Abu Dhabi
details: 174 ballistic missiles fired (161 intercepted), 8 cruise missiles (all intercepted), 689 drones (645 intercepted, 44 hit). 3 foreign nationals killed (Pakistani, Nepalese, Bangladeshi). Drone intercepted near Etihad Towers.

name: UAE — Al Minhad Air Base (Australian Forces)
details: Around 50 Australian military personnel attacked by Iranian drone strike at Al Minhad Air Base south of Dubai. All Australian forces declared safe.

name: Dubai — Port Jebel Ali
details: Smoke from missile intercepts seen over Dubai. Dark plumes rising from the Port of Jebel Ali, one of the world's busiest ports.

name: Saudi Arabia — Riyadh
details: Iran targeted Riyadh and the eastern region. Saudi Foreign Ministry confirmed attacks were repelled. Riyadh hit by direct strikes in second wave.

name: Saudi Arabia — Ras Tanura Refinery
details: Aramco's Ras Tanura refining facility damaged, causing a contained fire. Key oil infrastructure targeted.

name: Jordan — Intercepted Strikes
details: Jordanian armed forces intercepted 49 drones and ballistic missiles entering Jordanian airspace. German troops in Jordan also shelled by Iran.

name: Iraq — Erbil Airport Drone Attack
details: Explosion near Erbil Airport following attack by Iraqi militia drones aligned with Iran. US/Israel also struck the Jurf al-Sakher base in southern Iraq.

name: Iraq — Jurf al-Sakher Base
details: US and Israel targeted the Jurf al-Sakher base in southern Iraq, an Iranian proxy militia stronghold.

name: Cyprus — UK Military Bases
details: UK Defence Secretary confirmed Iran fired 2 missiles towards British bases on Cyprus. IRGC general threatened to strike the island with such intensity that Americans would be forced to leave.

name: Ramat David Airbase (Hezbollah)
details: Hezbollah fired missiles and a 'swarm of drones' at Ramat David airbase near Haifa — first cross-border raid since late 2024. Part of retaliation for killing of Khamenei.

name: Meron Monitoring Base (Hezbollah)
details: Hezbollah missiles and drones targeted the Meron monitoring/surveillance base in northern Israel.

name: Beirut — Southern Suburbs (Dahieh)
details: Israel bombed Beirut's southern suburbs after Hezbollah rocket attack. Hezbollah intelligence chief Hussein Makled killed. Palestinian Islamic Jihad commander also killed. 30,000+ displaced.

name: Southern Lebanon — Ground Incursion
details: Israeli military announced troops 'operating in southern Lebanon' in a new ground incursion described as 'forward defence'. Evacuation warnings issued for 50+ towns. Previously, 397 killed since ceasefire.

name: Strait of Hormuz — Tanker 'Skylight'
details: Palau-flagged oil tanker 'Skylight' targeted north of the port of Khasab, Oman. Iran closed the Strait of Hormuz, trapping 200+ vessels including oil and LNG tankers.

name: Off Coast of Oman — Tanker 'MKD VYOM'
details: Marshall Islands-flagged tanker 'MKD VYOM' struck off the coast of Oman. One crew member killed.

name: USS Abraham Lincoln — Missile Attack
details: IRGC claimed it launched 4 ballistic missiles at the US aircraft carrier USS Abraham Lincoln, saying retaliatory strikes had entered a 'new phase'. Outcome disputed.

name: Persian Gulf — Iranian Navy Destroyed
details: CENTCOM stated that the US destroyed 17 Iranian ships, including Iran's most operational submarine and the drone carrier Shahid Bagheri. Total of 20 Iranian vessels sunk.

name: Qatar Airspace — Iranian Su-24s Shot Down
details: Qatar shot down 2 Iranian Su-24 bombers — first nation to shoot down Iranian aircraft in the conflict.

name: Kuwait Airspace — US F-15 Crashes
details: 3 US F-15 fighters crashed in Kuwait. Iran claimed to have shot them down; CENTCOM attributed it to friendly fire by Kuwait.

name: Tehran — Mehrabad Airport (2nd Strike)
details: Mehrabad Airport struck again on Day 5. Mehr News Agency published photos showing smoke rising behind a runway. 11th wave of strikes in Tehran since start of conflict.

name: Shiraz — Air Force Base (2nd Strike)
details: Iranian Air Force base in Shiraz struck again. IAF carried out hundreds of sorties, dropping over 5,000 bombs across Iran during the conflict.

name: Tabriz — Police Building (2nd Strike)
details: Police building in Tabriz struck again as part of continuing multi-province operations targeting Basij and security infrastructure.

name: Tehran — F-35 Shoots Down Yak-130
details: Israeli F-35I 'Adir' shot down an Iranian Air Force Yak-130 over Tehran — first confirmed air-to-air kill by an F-35 and first downing of an enemy fighter by Israel since 1985.

name: Indian Ocean — IRIS Dena Sunk (off Sri Lanka)
details: US Navy submarine torpedoed Iranian frigate IRIS Dena with a Mark 48 torpedo ~40nm south of Galle, Sri Lanka. Ship sank in minutes. First submarine sinking of a surface vessel since the Falklands War (1982). 32 survivors rescued.

name: Central Israel — Ballistic Missile Waves
details: Eight Iranian attack waves against Israel including ballistic missiles. Two waves were coordinated simultaneous launches from Iran and Lebanon toward central Israel. Sirens in Jerusalem, Tel Aviv, and across central Israel.

name: Kuwait — 11-year-old Killed by Shrapnel
details: An 11-year-old Iranian national killed by shrapnel from Iranian strikes on Kuwait. Kuwait under daily retaliatory attacks since the war began.

name: Bahrain — Amazon Data Center
details: Iran launched a drone attack on an Amazon data center in Bahrain, according to state-affiliated Iranian news agency.

name: Eastern Mediterranean — NATO Intercepts Missile
details: NATO air defense systems shot down an Iranian ballistic missile heading toward Turkish airspace over the Eastern Mediterranean. USS Oscar Austin (DDG-79) identified as interceptor. First NATO interception defending a member state in this conflict.

name: Tel Aviv Area — Hezbollah Rockets
details: Hezbollah expanded range of attacks to Tel Aviv area. 38 attacks over 24 hours using drones, anti-tank missiles, and rockets. Two coordinated waves with Iran toward central Israel.
casualties: Damage to residential buildings, injuries reported

name: Haifa — Hezbollah Missile Attacks
details: Hezbollah launched missiles toward Haifa area as part of escalating campaign. Alerts activated across the Galilee Panhandle.

name: Beirut — Hotel Strike & Eastern Lebanon
details: Israeli strikes hit a hotel in a Beirut suburb and a residential complex in eastern Lebanon. Total Lebanese death toll reached 77 killed, 527 injured since Monday.

name: Southern Lebanon — Rocket Sites Destroyed
details: IDF completed wave of strikes against Hezbollah targets south of the Litani River — rocket/missile launch sites and a drone manufacturing lab destroyed. Israeli forces advanced deeper into southern Lebanon.

name: Mariwan — Kurdish Ground Offensive
details: Reports of thousands of Kurdish PJAK fighters launching ground offensive into NW Iran. Iranian forces evacuated border city of Mariwan. CIA reportedly arming Kurdish forces. Conflicting reports — PJAK and Iran denied the offensive.

name: Tehran — Azadi Sports Complex
details: Tehran's iconic Azadi sports complex hit by US-Israeli attack. The 12,000-capacity indoor multi-purpose stadium was struck. Part of intensifying Day 6 campaign.

name: Tehran — Golestan Palace (UNESCO Site)
details: UNESCO-listed Golestan Palace struck according to IRNA. A children's park in Tehran also hit. Authorities say US-Israeli attacks have hit dozens of civilian centers including hospitals, schools, and heritage sites.

name: Tehran — Residential & Police Targets
details: Explosions in west and northwest Tehran. Thick smoke rising from various areas. Police stations and residential areas struck. Continued Day 6 operations.

name: Nakhchivan Airport — Iranian Drones (Azerbaijan)
details: Iranian Shahed-type drones struck Nakhchivan International Airport passenger terminal and near a school in Shakarabad village. Azerbaijan's defense ministry confirmed four drones. Iran denied involvement. President Aliyev demanded apology; army placed on full combat readiness.

name: Qatar — Doha Missile Barrage (Largest Yet)
details: Largest and longest Iranian missile barrage on Doha — 14 ballistic missiles and 4 drones fired. 13 missiles intercepted; 1 fell in territorial waters. Evacuation ordered near US Embassy. Qatar's LNG exports shut down, threatening energy crisis.

name: Kuwait — Camp Buehring Drone Attack
details: Iran launched drone attack on Camp Buehring (formerly Udairi), major US military facility in NW Kuwait. Footage captured moment of impact.

name: Iraq — US Base near Baghdad Airport
details: Iranian drones targeted US military base near Baghdad International Airport as part of wider regional strikes on Day 6.

name: Israel — Overnight Missile Waves (Mar 5)
details: Iran launched three waves of missiles toward Israel overnight. Sirens sounded in Tel Aviv and Jerusalem. Interceptor activity reported across central Israel.

name: Beirut — Dahiyeh Mass Evacuation & Strikes
details: At least six Israeli airstrikes slammed into Dahiyeh (southern suburbs) at dawn. IDF issued unprecedented mass evacuation warning covering entire neighborhoods — significant expansion from previous building-specific orders.
casualties: Part of 77 total killed in Lebanon since Monday

name: Northern Israel — Hezbollah Continued Attacks
details: Hezbollah continued rocket and drone attacks on northern Israel communities. Ongoing campaign of 38+ daily attacks expanded to include anti-tank missiles and longer-range rockets.

name: Isfahan — Ammunition Depot & Military Installations
details: Bunker-buster bombs dropped on mountainous area near Isfahan targeting ammunition depot and air base. Caused massive secondary explosions. Shockwaves damaged UNESCO World Heritage sites including Naqsh-e Jahan Square and Shah Mosque.
casualties: Military casualties; significant cultural heritage damage

name: Isfahan — IRGC Senior Commander Strike
details: Israeli Air Force strike in Isfahan eliminated senior IRGC official Haj Ali Hashem.

name: Bandar Abbas — Naval Base & IRGC Navy Command
details: Strikes on Bandar Abbas port destroyed Iranian navy ship Bandar Lengeh. Eliminated IRGC Navy commander Ali Reza Tangsiri, Navy intelligence chief Behnam Rezaei, and First Fleet commander General Masib Bakhtiari.
casualties: 3+ senior naval commanders killed, ship destroyed

name: Kermanshah — IRGC Air Force HQ
details: Israeli Air Force struck IRGC Air Force headquarters and Space and Satellite Command facilities in Kermanshah.

name: Qom — Ammunition Bunkers
details: Strikes destroyed approximately 50 ammunition bunkers in Qom province.

name: Shiraz — IRGC & Electronics Factory
details: Strikes hit Fars provincial command headquarters, Shiraz International Airport military section, and an electronics factory.

name: Tabriz — Governor's Building & Ministry of Intelligence
details: Strikes hit the governor's building and Ministry of Intelligence offices in Tabriz.

name: Tabriz — Basij Checkpoint
details: Airstrike on a Basij checkpoint in Tabriz killed 13 Basij fighters and injured 18 others.

name: Sanandaj — Shahramfar IRGC Base
details: Israeli Air Force struck Shahramfar IRGC base in Kurdistan province.

name: Khorramabad — IRGC Warehouses
details: Strikes hit warehouses and military facilities in Khorramabad, Lorestan province.

name: Karaj — Basij Base Mohammadshahr
details: Israeli Air Force struck Basij base in Mohammadshahr district of Karaj, near Tehran.

name: Tehran — IRGC Quds Force Headquarters
details: Israeli Air Force struck Quds Force headquarters in Tehran. Part of 170+ munitions delivered by dozens of fighter jets across Tehran, Isfahan, and Shiraz.
casualties: Part of cumulative ~1,900 Iranian regime members eliminated by this date

name: Parchin — Military Complex
details: Continued strikes on Parchin Military Complex southeast of Tehran, targeting underground facilities used for drone and missile manufacturing.

name: Kish Island — Airport Fuel Depot
details: Strike destroyed fuel depot at Kish Island airport.

name: Kharg Island — Military Sites Bombing Raid
details: USAF conducted large-scale precision bombing raid on Kharg Island, Iran's key oil export hub handling 90% of oil exports. Struck 90+ military targets. Oil infrastructure intentionally spared.
casualties: Military casualties not specified; 15+ explosions reported

name: Isfahan — Heating/Cooling Equipment Factory
details: Missile strike on industrial area hit a factory producing heating and cooling equipment while workers were inside. Iranian Ministry of Health reported cumulative toll of 1,444 killed and 18,500+ wounded since war began.

name: Yehud (Tel Aviv area) — Cluster Munition Strike
details: Iranian ballistic missile with cluster bomb warhead struck near Yehud. Submunitions hit two construction workers outdoors. Human Rights Watch condemned strikes as potential war crimes.

name: Tel Aviv Metro Area — Ballistic Missile Barrage
details: 12 attack waves recorded on March 9, with 33% targeting central/Tel Aviv area. Over 3,000 residents displaced.

name: Northern Israel — Iranian Missile Waves
details: Continued Iranian ballistic missile attacks. By March 10, Iranian missile and drone attacks had dropped by more than 90% due to US/Israeli suppression of launch sites inside Iran.
casualties: Cumulative by Mar 10: 14 Israeli civilians killed, 2,339 injured total

name: Haifa & Upper Galilee — Hezbollah Rocket Barrage
details: Hezbollah fired rockets at northern Israel, triggering sirens in Haifa and Upper Galilee region.

name: Southern Lebanon — IDF Vehicle Attack
details: Hezbollah rocket struck IDF Puma armored vehicle, then hit a Caterpillar D9 rescue bulldozer. 31 attack waves conducted on this day.

name: Ramla — Fateh-110 Missile Strike on Kindergarten
details: Hezbollah Fateh-110 missile (~350km range, ~500kg warhead) struck a kindergarten in the Ramla civilian area. 16 wounded including 14 from the daycare.

name: Golan Heights — Hezbollah Rockets and Artillery
details: Series of attacks with rockets and artillery shells targeting Israeli military positions in Golan Heights. Campaign total by this date: 269 attack waves since March 2.

name: Beirut — Hezbollah HQ in High-Rise Buildings
details: Israeli airstrikes targeted Hezbollah headquarters in ten high-rise buildings in Beirut, as well as drone storage warehouses.
casualties: 8 Israeli soldiers injured in related border operations

name: Al-Qaouzah — UNIFIL Battalion HQ
details: Two Israeli missiles struck the UNIFIL (UN peacekeeping) battalion headquarters at Al-Qaouzah near Bint Jbeil.

name: Nabi Chit, Eastern Lebanon — Hezbollah Positions
details: Extensive airstrikes on village of Nabi Chit. At least 3 Lebanese Armed Forces soldiers and 1 General Security member among the casualties.

name: Beirut — Ramada Hotel (Iranian Quds Force Commanders)
details: Airstrike on Ramada hotel in central Beirut targeting Iranian Quds Force commanders. Killed 5 senior Quds Force Lebanon Corps officers, 10 wounded.

name: Beirut Dahiya — Radwan Unit HQ & Training Compound
details: Over 100 strikes across Lebanon. Radwan Unit (Hezbollah special forces) headquarters and training compound struck. ~30 Hezbollah members eliminated including 10 Radwan fighters. Commander Abu Hussein Ra'ghab killed.

name: Al-Qlayaa — Residential Airstrike
details: Double airstrike on a residential house in Al-Qlayaa killed a Maronite Catholic priest, Father Pierre al-Rahi, by Israeli tank fire. 4 others wounded.

name: Southern Lebanon — Iranian Commander Elimination
details: Overnight airstrikes confirmed elimination of Iranian commander Ali Musallam Tabaja.

name: Ain Ebel — Village Outskirts Airstrike
details: Israeli airstrike on outskirts of Ain Ebel village in southern Lebanon.

name: Burj Qalaouiyah — Health Center Airstrike
details: Israeli airstrike destroyed a health center, killing nearly the entire medical team. 4 additional staff listed as missing.

name: Bourj Hammoud, Beirut — Apartment Building Strike
details: Airstrike on apartment building in Bourj Hammoud, a densely populated Armenian neighborhood in northeastern Beirut.

name: Strait of Hormuz — Tugboat MUSAFFAH 2 Sunk
details: UAE-flagged tugboat MUSAFFAH 2 struck by two missiles and sank. 4 survived, 3 Indonesian crew missing/presumed dead.

name: Persian Gulf — Oil Tanker Prima Drone Strike
details: IRGC struck oil tanker Prima with a drone in the Persian Gulf. Same day, US oil tanker Louis P struck in the Strait of Hormuz.

name: Strait of Hormuz — Three Cargo Ships Attacked
details: IRGC struck three commercial ships. Thai-flagged Mayuree Naree engulfed in flames, 20 crew rescued, 3 missing.

name: Al-Udeid Air Base, Qatar — Iranian UAV Strike
details: Iranian UAV struck Al-Udeid Air Base, the largest US military facility in the Middle East.

name: Kuwait International Airport — Aviation Fuel Tanks
details: Iranian strike hit aviation fuel storage tanks at Kuwait International Airport.

name: Bahrain — Bapco Oil Refinery UAV Strike
details: Iranian UAV struck Bapco national oil refinery in Bahrain. Explosion near Manama refinery injured 32, 4 seriously.

name: Tehran — Larijani Assassination Strike
details: Israeli airstrike killed Ali Larijani, Secretary of Iran's Supreme National Security Council, along with his son Morteza, office head Alireza Bayat, and several guards. Gen. Gholam Reza Soleimani (Basij) also killed in separate strike same day.

name: Natanz — Uranium Enrichment Facility
details: US B-2 bombers dropped bunker-buster bombs on Natanz Fuel Enrichment Plant. IAEA confirmed entrance buildings damaged. No radiological effects reported.

name: Tehran — Eastern Districts IRGC Targets
details: Described as 'unprecedented' wave of strikes. Massive explosions on eastern side of Tehran. Multiple waves on IRGC infrastructure. Electricity outages across multiple areas.

name: Qom — Turbine Engine Production Site
details: US military struck a turbine engine production site used for drone and aircraft components linked to the IRGC.

name: Shiraz — 7th Tactical Airbase
details: Combined force strike on Artesh Air Force 7th Tactical Airbase co-located with Shiraz International Airport.

name: Bandar Abbas — 9th Airbase Ammunition Bunkers
details: CENTCOM struck ammunition bunkers at the 9th Artesh Air Force Tactical Airbase with at least four strikes followed by a large secondary explosion. IRGC 1st Naval District Commander Mosayeb Bakhtiari also killed.
casualties: At least 1 senior IRGC commander killed

name: Yazd — Ardakan Yellowcake Production Plant
details: Israeli Air Force struck uranium processing facility described as a 'unique facility' in Iran's nuclear infrastructure. No radiation leaks reported.

name: Arak — Shahid Khondab Heavy Water Complex
details: Israeli airstrikes damaged the heavy water reactor complex. IAEA assessed the facility 'sustained severe damage and is no longer operational.'

name: Bushehr — Near Nuclear Power Plant
details: A projectile hit near the Bushehr Nuclear Power Plant. Iran's Atomic Energy Organization confirmed no casualties or radiation leaks.

name: Bandar Khamir — Port Quay
details: Joint US-Israeli strikes hit a quay at the port city near the Strait of Hormuz.

name: Isfahan — Military Installations (Mountains)
details: Heavy bunker-buster bombs hit Army ammo depot near Mount Soffeh, triggering massive secondary explosions that lit up the night sky.

name: Jerusalem — Old City / Temple Mount Debris
details: Missile debris and shrapnel damaged religious sites including Temple Mount/Al-Aqsa Mosque area, Church of the Holy Sepulchre, and the Jewish Quarter.

name: Ramat Gan — Residential Building Cluster Strike
details: Cluster warhead missile struck a residential building, killing two residents in their 70s found outside their safe room. IRGC called it revenge for Larijani assassination.

name: Beit Awwa — Bridal Salon (West Bank)
details: Iranian cluster bomb struck a bridal salon. Four Palestinian women killed while preparing food for a Ramadan meal. 13 others injured. First Iranian attack to kill Palestinians.

name: Ben Gurion Airport — Missile Debris
details: IRGC fired Khorramshahr-4 missiles with one-ton warheads at the airport. Three private planes damaged by falling debris from intercepted missiles.
casualties: Planes damaged, no casualties at airport

name: Jerusalem & Northern Israel — 14 Salvos
details: Iran fired 14 salvos within one hour at Jerusalem and northern Israel. Interceptor debris caused injuries in Tel Aviv.

name: Dimona — Near Negev Nuclear Research Center
details: Retaliatory strike after US bombing of Natanz. At least two ballistic missiles evaded air defenses. IAEA confirmed no damage to nuclear facility.

name: Arad — Residential Areas
details: Part of retaliatory wave targeting southern Israel near the nuclear site. Ballistic missile struck residential building. 11 seriously injured.

name: Tel Aviv — Residential Building Cluster Strike
details: Cluster munition strike on residential building killed a security guard in an unprotected area.

name: Haifa / Upper Galilee — Major Barrage
details: Hezbollah fired over 30 rockets at Haifa and Nahariya.

name: Nahariya — Rocket Strike
details: Hezbollah rocket killed one Israeli civilian in Nahariya during attacks on advancing IDF positions.

name: Haifa — Oil Refinery Strike
details: Rocket struck an oil tanker at the Haifa refinery, causing a large fire and black smoke plume. Fire fully contained, no hazardous material leak.

name: Haifa / Galilee — 45-Rocket Barrage
details: Hezbollah fired approximately 45 rockets at northern Israel, triggering sirens in Haifa, Safed, and across the Galilee.

name: Southern Lebanon — Ground Invasion Launch
details: IDF announced 'targeted ground operation against key targets' in southern Lebanon.

name: Beirut — Bashoura, Zuqaq Al Blat Districts
details: Extensive overnight airstrikes on central Beirut with no advance warnings. At least 5 attacks in 12 hours. Al-Manar TV director Mohammad Shari and his wife killed. 10-storey building destroyed.

name: Litani River — Qasmiyeh Bridge
details: Israel destroyed the Qasmiyeh Bridge, the main bridge over the Litani River, to cut off Hezbollah supply routes.
casualties: 1 Israeli civilian killed (friendly fire from IDF artillery)

name: Southern Lebanon — Ground Combat Zone
details: Hezbollah attacks on advancing IDF forces. Two IDF soldiers killed in ground combat operations as Israel expanded buffer zone.

name: Southern Lebanon — Journalist Strike
details: Israeli airstrike killed two journalists: Ali Choeib (Al-Manar TV) and Fatima Ftouni (Al Mayadeen).

name: Bint Jbeil — Paramedic Strike
details: Israeli airstrike killed two Lebanese paramedics. One IDF soldier killed, three wounded in separate ground operations.
casualties: 2 paramedics killed; 1 IDF soldier killed, 3 wounded

name: Fujairah — Gas Al Ahmadiah Tanker Hit
details: Kuwait-flagged LPG tanker Gas Al Ahmadiah hit by a projectile while anchored east of Fujairah.

name: Khor Fakkan — Parimal Chemical Tanker Fire
details: Palau-flagged chemical tanker Parimal struck, sparking a fire. Fifteen crew evacuated; captain reported missing.

name: Kuwait — Sonangol Namibe Tanker Sea Drone
details: Sea drone struck the oil tanker Sonangol Namibe (Angolan state-owned) anchored near port. Caused oil spill posing environmental risk.

name: Dubai — Al Salmi VLCC Tanker Drone Attack
details: Iranian drone struck the Kuwait VLCC tanker Al Salmi loaded with 2 million barrels of oil while anchored at Dubai port. Fire contained. All 24 crew safe.

name: Bandar Abbas — Shahid Haqqani Passenger Pier
details: Fighter jets bombed civilian port infrastructure at Shahid Haqqani passenger pier in Hormozgan province.

name: Qeshm Island — Desalination Plant
details: Water desalination facility struck and rendered non-operational, cutting off critical water infrastructure.

name: Isfahan — Steel Plants
details: Steel manufacturing plants in Isfahan struck as part of industrial targeting campaign.

name: Tehran — Pasteur Institute
details: Century-old medical research center struck. One of Iran's most prominent scientific institutions dating back to 1920.

name: Bushehr — Red Crescent Warehouse
details: US drone strike hit a Red Crescent humanitarian aid warehouse in Bushehr province.

name: Bushehr — Nuclear Power Plant (Auxiliary Building)
details: Projectile struck auxiliary building of Iran's only operational nuclear power plant. IAEA confirmed no radiation increase. Physical protection staff member killed. 198 Russian Rosatom staff evacuated.

name: Mahshahr — Special Petrochemical Zone
details: Airstrikes hit Iran's key Mahshahr petrochemical complex in Khuzestan province, a major industrial hub and source of export revenue.

name: Ardabil Province — Jafarabad & Germi
details: Air strikes on Jafarabad and Germi near Iran's northwestern border with Azerbaijan.

name: Tehran — Shahid Beheshti University
details: Section of major university destroyed in air raids.

name: Petah Tikva — Aero Sol Drone Factory
details: Iranian hypersonic missile struck near the fence of Aero Sol Aviation Solutions factory, a defense contractor. Facility declared 'total loss.' Large crater at impact site.

name: Tel Aviv Area — Missile Barrage
details: Iranian missile barrage on central Israel. 14 people wounded including an 11-year-old girl. Coordinated with Houthi missiles.

name: Tel Aviv — IDF Kirya HQ Vicinity
details: Two cluster submunitions from an Iranian ballistic missile struck near the IDF's Kirya headquarters. Damage to a parking lot and nearby school.

name: Haifa — Residential Building Collapse
details: Iranian ballistic missile warhead struck a six-story residential building causing partial collapse. Four residents killed in stairwell while trying to reach shelter.

name: Tel Aviv / Central Israel — Cluster Missile Barrage
details: Waves of Iranian cluster missiles hit dozens of locations across the Tel Aviv area. Coordinated with Houthi and Hezbollah attacks.

name: Tel Aviv Area — Overnight Cluster Strike
details: Cluster munition struck multiple locations in the Tel Aviv metropolitan area overnight.

name: Kiryat Ata — Hezbollah Rocket Barrage
details: Hezbollah fired barrage of over 30 rockets at Israeli military infrastructure east of Haifa in minutes.

name: Kiryat Shmona — Rocket Without Warning
details: Rocket fell in Kiryat Shmona residential area without warning sirens. Part of 51 total Hezbollah attacks over the weekend.

name: Acre Area — UAV Strike on Home
details: Hezbollah UAV struck a residential home in a community near Acre in the Galilee.

name: Nabatieh — Family Home Strike
details: Israeli strike killed an entire family: a man, his wife, and two daughters.

name: Ramadiyeh — Tyre District
details: Israeli military strike on the town of Ramadiyeh in Tyre district.

name: Kfarsir — Nabatieh District
details: Early morning strike on a two-story building in Kfarsir.

name: Habbush — Southern Lebanon
details: Israeli strike killed two girls and wounded 22 people.

name: Tyre — Hospital Vicinity Strike
details: Strike wounded 11 people. Overnight strikes destroyed two buildings near the Lebanese Italian Hospital, shattering windows and collapsing ceilings inside. Hospital remained operational.

name: Bekaa Valley — Sohmor & Mashghara Bridges
details: Israel destroyed two critical bridges over the Litani River in the western Bekaa Valley, cutting off Hezbollah supply routes.

name: Kfar Hatta — Southern Sidon District
details: Air attacks launched hours after evacuation orders. Strike killed seven people including a 4-year-old girl and a Lebanese Army soldier.

name: Maarakeh — Southern Lebanon
details: Israeli bombing of the town of Maarakeh. Including two women killed at a junction.

name: Beirut — Jnah Neighborhood (Southern Suburbs)
details: Multiple strikes on Beirut's southern suburbs. Strike hit approximately 100 meters from Rafik Hariri University Hospital.

name: Central Iran — US F-15E Shot Down
details: Iran shot down a US F-15E using 'a new advanced defence system.' Both crew ejected. One rescued Apr 3, second evaded capture for 2 days and rescued Apr 5 in dramatic nighttime operation.

name: Strait of Hormuz — MSC Ishika Container Ship
details: IRGC Navy drone struck the MSC Ishika, an Israeli-linked container vessel, setting it ablaze.

name: Abu Dhabi — Habshan Gas Plant
details: Falling debris from intercepted Iranian strikes caused two fires at Habshan gas plant. One Egyptian national killed during evacuation.

name: Kuwait — Power & Water Desalination Plants
details: Iranian drone attacks damaged two power and water desalination plants and shut down two electricity generating units.

name: Kuwait — Petroleum Corporation Facilities
details: Iranian drone attack on Kuwait Petroleum Corporation caused significant material losses and fires.

name: Bahrain — Bapco Oil Tank Fire
details: Iranian drone attack set fire to a Bapco Energies oil storage tank. Fire extinguished.

name: Southern Suburbs of Beirut — Israeli Strikes
details: Large wave of Israeli strikes

name: Southern Lebanon — Israeli Strikes
details: Large wave of Israeli strikes

name: Eastern Bekaa Valley — Israeli Strikes
details: Large wave of Israeli strikes

name: Lavan Island — Oil Refinery
details: Missile attack on oil refinery

name: Lebanon — Fresh Israeli Attacks
details: Several people reported killed
